  "category_focus": "R1",

  "clarification_question": "Should the check focus on (A) technical claims—logical consistency, alignment of claims with the reported results, and avoiding overclaims; (B) writing polish—grammar, clarity, and tone; or (C) conceptual/positioning—reframing the thesis and contribution? Please pick A, B, or C.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The request plausibly entails validating the internal logic from setup to conclusions in the abstract.",
    "R1.3": "The text makes quantitative claims that should be checked against the reported numbers and comparisons.",
    "R1.4": "The abstract asserts novelty and advances over prior routing approaches, warranting a delta-versus-prior assessment.",
    "RX.1": "Any check should note uncertainties and what cannot be verified from the excerpt alone.",
    "RX.2": "The review must flag or avoid endorsing unsupported or inflated claims relative to the presented evidence.",
    "RX.3": "The work should stay within the scope of checking the provided abstract without expanding to tasks not requested.",
    "RX.4": "Ensure the evaluation and phrasing do not encourage misleading deployment claims or misuse.",
    "RX.5": "All judgments should be tied explicitly to statements and figures present in the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the check focus on (A) technical claims—logical consistency, alignment of claims with the reported results, and avoiding overclaims; (B) writing polish—grammar, clarity, and tone; or (C) conceptual/positioning—reframing the thesis and contribution? Please pick A, B, or C.

rationale.R1.2: The request plausibly entails validating the internal logic from setup to conclusions in the abstract.
rationale.R1.3: The text makes quantitative claims that should be checked against the reported numbers and comparisons.
rationale.R1.4: The abstract asserts novelty and advances over prior routing approaches, warranting a delta-versus-prior assessment.
rationale.RX.1: Any check should note uncertainties and what cannot be verified from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review must flag or avoid endorsing unsupported or inflated claims relative to the presented evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The work should stay within the scope of checking the provided abstract without expanding to tasks not requested.
rationale.RX.4: Ensure the evaluation and phrasing do not encourage misleading deployment claims or misuse.
rationale.RX.5: All judgments should be tied explicitly to statements and figures present in the provided text.
